data.text: There's already an `untrusted.go` file. Let me read it and the relevant prompt-building code.

data.output:
1	package evidence
2	
3	// Canonical boundary markers for prompt assembly when embedding
4	// untrusted artifact bodies.
5	const (
6		UntrustedDataOpen  = "<untrusted-data>"
7		UntrustedDataClose = "</untrusted-data>"
8	)
9	
10	// DelimitUntrustedData wraps body in a canonical untrusted-data envelope so
11	// prompt renderers can distinguish trusted instructions from artifact text.
12	func DelimitUntrustedData(body string) string {
13		return UntrustedDataOpen + "\n" + body + "\n" + UntrustedDataClose
14	}
15

=== .ddx/executions/20260507T041817-4dfae0a2/no_changes_rationale.txt ===
The spec changes for bead ddx-cf37253d were already committed in a prior
execution attempt as commit 92ed74563 ("docs: define DDx temp cleanup scope
and thresholds [ddx-cf37253d]"). That commit is an ancestor of the current
base revision 6f771abaf and is present in the main branch.

The commit addressed all four content ACs:
- AC1: FEAT-010 §"Execution Resource Cleanup" now names DDx-owned cleanup
  scope beyond execution worktrees (test/e2e scratch roots, generated test
  binaries, run-state/liveness files) and states the conservative
  ownership/liveness rules (DDx-owned paths with ownership metadata or known
  DDx prefixes, minimum age/mtime, no live PID/session liveness).
- AC2: SD-025 §"Resource preflight and loop-fatal handling" defines the soft
  high-water threshold trigger and hard floor stop behavior for ddx work and
  server-managed workers before each claim.
- AC3: reliability-principles.md P8 was updated to explicitly name leaked
  DDx test/e2e scratch roots as within the reclamation scope.
- AC4: Both FEAT-010 and SD-025 name the three expected implementation tests:
  TestExecutionCleanup_RemovesStaleDDXScratchDirs,
  TestWorkResourcePreflight_RunsCleanupBelowSoftFloor, and
  TestWorkResourcePreflight_StopsBelowHardFloorAfterCleanup.

The execution worktree for this attempt was removed by a background cleanup

input.content:
verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/... -run TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...

All five acceptance criteria are satisfied by code already present in the tree at base revision d130fbae9:

AC1 — Reusable delimiter helper:
  cli/internal/evidence/untrusted.go defines `DelimitUntrustedData(body string) string`
  and exports `UntrustedDataOpen = "<untrusted-data>"` / `UntrustedDataClose = "</untrusted-data>"`.

AC2 — Helper used at every artifact-body interpolation site in scope:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:375 wraps governing-doc content bodies:
    fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(txt))
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:397 wraps diff content:
    fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(...))
  No other artifact-body interpolation sites exist in the server or agent packages.

AC3 — TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_evidence_test.go:204 runs the named test.
  It asserts:
    - docExpected := evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(payload) appears in the rendered prompt
    - diffExpected := evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(...diff...) appears in the rendered prompt
    - evidence.UntrustedDataOpen and UntrustedDataClose are both present
    - the raw payload is still preserved inside the wrapper (injection boundary is additive, not destructive)

AC4 — cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/... passes:
  Verified in this execution: all packages returned ok (server: 81s, server/graphql: 8s,
  agent: 28s, agent/escalation, agent/executeloop, agent/try, agent/work, agent/workerprobe).

AC5 — lefthook run pre-commit passes:
  Verified in this execution: all hooks passed (no matching staged files = correct for no-change pass).

The bead's work was fully implemented in an earlier iteration; this pass confirms the contract
is satisfied and the verification command exits 0.
